//: LubbDubb example config. Copy to lubbdubb.config.json (gitignored). Every key is OPTIONAL — omit any to take its default. The '//'-prefixed keys are inline docs; they are ignored at load, so this file stays copy-and-run. As shipped it drives the mock agent (agentMode 'raw' + fake integrations), so `npm start` works with no model or provider auth. Precedence: explicit overrides > this file > defaults, plus env overrides (PORT, LUBBDUBB_DB, LUBBDUBB_REPO_ROOT).
// heartbeatIntervalMs: How often (ms) the heartbeat fires a dispatch cycle. Default 300000 (5 min).
// maxConcurrentAgents: Hard cap on concurrently-running agents; seeds the live runtime control. Adjustable without restart via POST /api/control; a restart reverts to this value. Default 3.
// startPaused: Boot with dispatch paused (no new agents until resumed). The only config-level pause knob — live pause/resume is runtime-only and ephemeral. Default false.
// dispatcher: Which dispatcher decides each cycle. 'rule' = deterministic, no model calls. 'claude' = an LLM decides (output still schema-validated), driven over a PTY. Default 'rule'.
// agentMode: How agents are launched. 'stream' = real Claude Code over headless stream-JSON (production default, runs unattended, supports the waiting/answer loop). 'pty' = real Claude Code as an interactive terminal (needs first-run onboarding done; this is the runtime that resumes across restarts). 'raw' = run claudeCommand/claudeArgs verbatim, prompt passed via LUBBDUBB_PROMPT (mock-agent demo + tests). Default 'stream'.
// claudeCommand: The agent binary to launch. Default 'claude'. Here it's 'bash' so the example can run the mock agent.
// claudeArgs: Extra args appended to the agent command. A relative script path is resolved to absolute at load (agents run in a worktree cwd). Default [].
// agentPermissionMode: Passed to `claude --permission-mode` so unattended tool calls don't hang. Default 'acceptEdits'. Note: 'bypassPermissions' maps to --dangerously-skip-permissions, which claude refuses under root.
// agentPromptDelayMs: PTY only. Wait this long (ms) after spawn before typing the task in, giving the interactive TUI time to boot. Ignored by stream/raw. Default 1200.
// agentSubmitDelayMs: PTY only. Gap (ms) between typing a message and sending its submitting carriage return — the claude TUI folds a burst into a paste and treats a glued-on CR as a literal newline, leaving the text unsubmitted. Set 0 to write both at once. Default 60.
// agentWaitingPatterns: Extra literal substrings that mean 'the CLI is waiting for input' (backup escalation heuristic). Default [].
// whitelistedApprovals: PTY waiting prompts the harness may auto-answer instead of escalating. Each rule: { match: <substring of the waiting prompt>, response: <text typed back> }. Default [].
// steeringPriorities: Optional ordered hints injected into the LLM dispatcher's prompt (only meaningful with dispatcher 'claude'). Default [].
// docsFolderPrefix: Folder(s) the file-events hook treats as the artifacts area: any file an agent writes UNDER a prefix is promoted to an artifact chip regardless of extension, on top of the built-in report/doc heuristic (report extensions + any reports/ folder). Accepts a string or an array; a file promotes under ANY entry. A relative entry is worktree-relative; an ABSOLUTE entry (e.g. D:/docs) also matches files written under that real directory OUTSIDE the worktree and lets the cockpit serve them. Unset = heuristic only.
// autoSend: Confidence-gated autonomy for side-effectful actions. Off by default: with enabled=false the harness always drafts + escalates for sign-off. Enabled, it sends an action itself only when the dispatcher's confidence >= confidenceThreshold AND the action type is in allowedActions; anything below still escalates, and a failed send falls back to an escalation. Deep-merged, so you can set just one field.

autoSend.// confidenceThreshold: Minimum dispatcher confidence (0..1) required to send instead of escalate. Default 0.85.
autoSend.// allowedActions: Action types eligible for auto-send. Default ['reply_on_pr'].

// integrations: Which provider fulfils each capability — the swap switch. One provider per capability, deep-merged so you can override just one. Providers: 'fake' (editable, injectable world — the demo default), 'github' + 'azure' (real, for sourceControl/issues), 'microsoft365' (calendar). Capabilities: sourceControl (pull requests), issues (issues/work items), backlog (stories), calendar (meetings).
// github: Target for the real 'github' provider — required only when integrations.sourceControl or .issues is 'github' (ignored otherwise). owner/repo name the repository; filters.prAuthor narrows the PR slice to one author. All open issues are ingested (the watch/ignore gate decides what's acted on, not an ingest filter). Auth is NOT here — set the GITHUB_TOKEN env var so a secret never lands in a committed file.
// azureDevOps: Target for the real 'azure' provider (Azure DevOps Repos + Boards) — required only when integrations.sourceControl or .issues is 'azure' (ignored otherwise). organization/project/repository name the repo; filters.prAuthor narrows PRs by uniqueName/UPN, filters.workItemTag narrows work items to one tag. Auth is NOT here — set AZURE_DEVOPS_PAT (Basic), or leave it unset to use the logged-in `az` CLI (Bearer).
// microsoft365: Target for the real 'microsoft365' provider (currently the calendar slice, via Microsoft Graph) — required only when integrations.calendar is 'microsoft365' (ignored otherwise). userId = target mailbox (UPN or object id), required for app-only tokens, omit for a delegated 'me' token. windowDays = how many days ahead to surface events (default 7). Auth is NOT here — set MICROSOFT_GRAPH_TOKEN, or leave unset to use the logged-in `az` CLI.
// labelPrefix: The prefix behind the cockpit's watch/ignore toggle, shared by PRs, issues and stories. It derives two labels: '<prefix>-watch' ('work this') and '<prefix>-ignore' ('leave this alone'). The no-tag default differs by type: PRs are opt-out (watched unless <prefix>-ignore), issues/stories are opt-in (ignored unless <prefix>-watch). Default 'lubbdubb', so 'lubbdubb-ignore' keeps its historical meaning as the PR exclusion tag. Set it to '' to disable both gates (PRs never excluded, all open issues/stories acted on).
// issuePickupRequireOwnLabel: Tighten the issue WATCH gate so '<labelPrefix>-watch' only counts when the account the harness authenticates as added it (anti-abuse). Only meaningful with a real provider (github/azure) that can resolve tag authorship; the fake provider fails the gate closed. Default false (any tagger counts).
// issuePriorityLabels: Label -> priority weight for ordering issue pickup when headroom is limited (higher = first). REPLACED wholesale by an override, not merged, so you can define your own scheme (e.g. p0/p1/p2). Default { 'priority:high': 3, 'priority:medium': 2, 'priority:low': 1 }.
// issueDefaultPriority: Weight for an issue carrying no matching priority label. Default 2.
// issuePickupStates: Dispatcher-level, state-based pickup gate. When non-empty, only issues whose provider-native workflow state is listed are picked up (e.g. ['Ready','Doing'] for Azure). Meaningful only for providers with a richer state model than open/closed (Azure work items); GitHub issues are unaffected. Unset/empty = no state gate.
// issueInReviewState: The state a work item is moved to once a PR is open for it, so agents stop re-picking work already awaiting review/CI (e.g. 'In Review'). Takes effect only alongside issuePickupStates and needs a provider that can write state back (Azure). Unset = no automatic transition.
// repoRoot: The git repo the harness operates on; per-branch worktrees are cut from it. Defaults to the launch directory (process.cwd()), so the common case needs nothing. Set it (or the LUBBDUBB_REPO_ROOT env override) to point at a repo elsewhere; a relative path resolves against the launch dir.
// worktreeRoot: Root under which per-branch worktrees are created. A relative value resolves against repoRoot (not the launch dir). Default '.lubbdubb/worktrees'.
// deskRoot: Root under which desk (no-code) scratch dirs are created. Relative resolves against repoRoot. Default '.lubbdubb/desk'.
// dbPath: SQLite file. Overridable via the LUBBDUBB_DB env var. Default '.lubbdubb/lubbdubb.sqlite'.
// port: HTTP/WS port for the server + cockpit. Overridable via the PORT env var. Default 4300.
